tu.prop: Whole-poem fallback because Chinese and English stanza structures do not align safely.

tuv.seg: 伯兮朅兮，邦之桀兮。伯也執殳，爲王前驅。 自伯之東，首如飛蓬。豈無膏沐？誰適爲容！ 其雨其雨，杲杲出日。願言思伯，甘心首疾。 焉得諼草？言樹之背。願言思伯。使我心痗。

1 My noble husband is how martial-like ! The hero of the country ! My husband, grasping his halberd, Is in the leading chariot of the king’s [host].
2 Since my husband went to the east, My head has been like the flying [pappus of the] artemisia. It is not that I could not anoint and wash it; But for whom should I adorn myself?
3 0 for rain ! 0 for rain ! But brightly the sun comes forth. Longingly I think of my husband, Till my heart is weary, and my head aches.
4 How shall I get the plant of forgetfulness ? I would plant it on the north of my house. Longingly I think of my husband, And my heart is made to ache.
